comment: New York two-tier regime: Banking Law §6-l high-cost (ineligible) and §6-m subprime (conditions), plus CEMA structure condition on refinances.

rules.description: New York high-cost home loan (§6-l): first-lien APR more than 8 points over comparable Treasury, or points and fees over 5% - high-cost loans are not originated (not GSE-deliverable)

New York subprime home loan (§6-m): first-lien rate more than 1.75 points over the Freddie Mac PMMS Northeast average triggers subprime duties (ability-to-repay verification, escrow, net tangible benefit, anti-steering)

artifact.text_template: NY §6-m subprime loan duties: document ability-to-repay verification, establish tax/insurance escrow, complete net-tangible-benefit analysis, and retain anti-steering documentation.

rules.description: New York refinance: CEMA (Consolidation, Extension & Modification Agreement) structure available so mortgage recording tax applies only to new money; underwriting standards unchanged - documentation/structure condition only
rules.citation: N.Y. Tax Law §255; Freddie Mac Guide 4101.11

artifact_always.text_template: Confirm whether the loan will close as a CEMA; if so, obtain prior-lender assignment and consolidation/extension/modification documents.
